reasoning.issue: The question asks what test or formula underlies the 'Cumulative Amount' (build-up/available amount) basket and when the basket begins to accrue.
reasoning.analysis: The Cumulative Amount basket is a general_purpose builder basket with a two-layer 'greatest of' architecture. The top-level capacity equals: (A) the greatest of three income-based tests (the 'three-test aggregate') PLUS (B) a fixed starter amount, plus additional additive sources. Layer 1 — The three-test aggregate (norm 6e76ed06_builder_source_three_test_aggregate, child_index 0) itself uses a 'greatest_of' among: (i) 50% of cumulative Consolidated Net Income since the anchor date (norm 6e76ed06_builder_source_cni); (ii) the Available Retained ECF Amount — the cumulative portion of Excess Cash Flow not required to prepay Term Loans under Section 2.10(f) (norm 6e76ed06_builder_source_ecf); and (iii) 140% of Consolidated EBITDA for the most recently ended Test Period (norm 6e76ed06_builder_source_ebitda_fc). Layer 2 — To that greatest-of figure is added (sum aggregation): the starter amount of the greater of $130,000,000 and 100% of Consolidated EBITDA (norm 6e76ed06_builder_source_starter); 100% of equity proceeds Not Otherwise Applied (norm 6e76ed06_builder_source_other_equity_proceeds); retained asset sale proceeds (norm 6e76ed06_builder_source_retained_asset_sale_proceeds); investment returns (norm 6e76ed06_builder_source_investment_returns); and debt-to-equity conversion amounts (norm 6e76ed06_builder_source_other_debt_conversion). The basket is reduced by prior RP usage, RDP usage, and investment usage (but not by unrestricted subsidiary designation). Start date: the v3 entity explicitly states the basket begins accruing from 'the first day of the fiscal quarter in which the Closing Date occurs' (growth_start_anchor = closing_date_fiscal_quarter_start).
reasoning.interactions: The three-test aggregate (6e76ed06_builder_source_three_test_aggregate) contributes to the top-level usage permission (6e76ed06_builder_usage_permission) via aggregation_function='greatest_of' at child_index 0, while all other sources (starter, equity proceeds, asset sale proceeds, investment returns, debt conversion) contribute via aggregation_function='sum'. The builder basket shares capacity with the builder_rdp_basket (Section 6.09(a)(A)), meaning RDP usage reduces available Cumulative Amount. No defeaters are present.
reasoning.conclusion: The Cumulative Amount basket is based on a 'greatest of' among (i) 50% of cumulative CNI, (ii) Available Retained ECF Amount, and (iii) 140% of LTM Consolidated EBITDA, plus additive sources including a $130M/100% EBITDA starter; it begins growing from the first day of the fiscal quarter in which the Closing Date occurs.

stage2.answer: The Cumulative Amount (build-up) basket is structured as a 'greatest of' three income-based tests — (i) 50% of cumulative Consolidated Net Income, (ii) the Available Retained ECF Amount (cumulative Excess Cash Flow not swept to prepay Term Loans under Section 2.10(f)), and (iii) 140% of Consolidated EBITDA for the most recently ended Test Period — plus the following additive sources summed on top: a starter amount equal to the greater of $130,000,000 and 100% of LTM Consolidated EBITDA, 100% of equity proceeds Not Otherwise Applied, retained asset sale proceeds, investment returns, and debt conversion amounts [Definition of Cumulative Amount, p. 32]. The basket begins accruing from 'the first day of the fiscal quarter in which the Closing Date occurs' [Definition of Cumulative Amount, p. 32]. Available capacity is reduced dollar-for-dollar by prior usage under the RP covenant (Section 6.06), the RDP covenant (Section 6.09(a)(A)), and the investment covenant, but is not reduced by unrestricted subsidiary designations [Definition of Cumulative Amount, p. 32].

questions.gold_answer: The Cumulative Amount is based on the greatest of three tests: (1) 50% of cumulative Consolidated Net Income (which amount shall not be less than zero for any fiscal quarter), (2) Excess Cash Flow not required to be applied to prepay Term Loans or any other debt (such amount cannot be less than $0 for any fiscal year), (3) cumulative Consolidated EBITDA minus 140% of cumulative Consolidated Fixed Charges. All tests start growing from the first day of the fiscal quarter in which the Closing Date occurs.

reasoning.issue: Whether the Borrower is permitted to dividend (distribute) the equity interests it holds in Unrestricted Subsidiaries to its equity holders or up the corporate chain.
reasoning.analysis: Section 6.06(p) contains an explicit, categorical permission (norm_id: 6e76ed06_unrestricted_sub_equity_distribution_permission) allowing 'the distribution, by dividend or otherwise, of shares of Equity Interests of... an Unrestricted Subsidiary or the proceeds thereof.' The v3 entity attributes confirm: is_categorical=true, capacity_category='categorical', covers_equity_interests=true, covers_proceeds=true, and partial_applicability=false. Crucially, requires_valid_designation=false, meaning no separate condition on the validity of the designation at the time of distribution is imposed. There are no defeaters listed against this norm. The basket also rebuilds the builder basket (rebuilds_builder_basket=true) and includes non-cash distributions at FMV (includes_noncash_at_fmv=true). Distributions can cascade (distributions_can_cascade=true), meaning a distribution of unsub equity up through intermediate entities is permissible. The J.Crew blocker (norm_id: 6e76ed06_jcrew_blocker_prohibition) applies only to designation of Restricted Subsidiaries as Unrestricted Subsidiaries holding Material Intellectual Property—it does not restrict the distribution of equity in already-designated Unrestricted Subsidiaries. No dollar cap, leverage condition, or other financial test applies to this specific basket. The general RP baskets (Sections 6.06(j) and 6.06(o)) and builder basket are also available for dividends more generally, but for unsub equity distributions specifically, Section 6.06(p) provides a standalone categorical carve-out that does not consume those baskets.
reasoning.interactions: The unsub distribution basket (6.06(p)) operates categorically and does not reduce the investment basket (reduces_investment_basket=false) or draw on the general RP or builder baskets. It does, however, rebuild the builder basket when used (rebuilds_builder_basket=true). No proceeds_flows or defeaters interact with this norm. The J.Crew blocker norm (6e76ed06_jcrew_blocker_prohibition) addresses designation mechanics, not distributions of already-designated unsub equity, and is therefore not in conflict.
reasoning.conclusion: Yes, the Borrower is categorically permitted under Section 6.06(p) to dividend equity interests in Unrestricted Subsidiaries, with no dollar cap, leverage test, or other financial condition required, and the permission extends to proceeds of such equity as well as non-cash distributions at FMV.

stage2.answer: Yes. Section 6.06(p) [p. 198] contains a categorical, uncapped permission for 'the distribution, by dividend or otherwise, of shares of Equity Interests of... an Unrestricted Subsidiary or the proceeds thereof,' with no dollar limit, leverage condition, or requirement that the subsidiary remain validly designated at the time of distribution. The basket covers equity interests, indebtedness owed by the unsub, assets, and proceeds thereof, and permits cascading distributions up through intermediate entities; non-cash distributions are valued at FMV. No defeaters apply to this norm, and use of this basket does not draw on or reduce the general RP or investment baskets (though it does rebuild the builder basket).

citations.quote: the distribution, by dividend or otherwise, of shares of Equity Interests of, or Indebtedness owed to Holdings or a Restricted Subsidiary by, or any assets of, an Unrestricted Subsidiary or the proceeds thereof
no Restricted Subsidiary may be designated as an Unrestricted Subsidiary if... such Unrestricted Subsidiary would own or have an exclusive license... to any Material Intellectual Property

questions.gold_answer: Yes, under 6.06(p) the Borrower can dividend shares of Equity Interest or any assets of an Unrestricted Subsidiary.

reasoning.issue: The question asks whether investment, restricted debt payment (RDP), or other baskets in the agreement can be reallocated and used to make restricted payments or dividends.
reasoning.analysis:
Three distinct reallocation pathways are present in the data:

1. **General RDP Basket → RP (fungible pool):** The general RDP basket under Section 6.09(a)(I) has `action_scope: 'reallocable'` and `capacity_composition: 'additive'`, capped at the greater of $130M and 100% of Consolidated EBITDA. The general RP basket under Section 6.06(j) also has `action_scope: 'reallocable'` and `capacity_composition: 'fungible'`, capped at the same greater-of $130M / 100% EBITDA amount. The fungible flag on the RP basket and the reallocable flag on both baskets indicate these two baskets share a common pool — capacity used for RDP reduces the RP basket and vice versa.

2. **Builder/Cumulative Amount (shared with RDP):** The builder RDP basket under Section 6.09(a)(A) has `shares_with_rp_builder: true`, meaning usage of the Cumulative Amount for RDP reduces the same pool available to make RPs and investments under Section 6.06 and Section 6.03(x). Conversely, the Cumulative Amount (builder basket) is explicitly available for RPs (dividends, equity repurchases), RDPs (subordinated debt prepayments), and investments — all drawing from the same pool. The builder basket is reduced by RP usage (`reduced_by_rp_usage: true`), RDP usage (`reduced_by_rdp_usage: true`), and investment usage (`reduced_by_investment_usage: true`).

3. **Investment Basket Section 6.03(y) → incorporates RDP capacity:** The general investment basket at Section 6.03(y) explicitly includes as part of its cap '(ii)(A) the aggregate total of all amounts available to be utilized for Restricted Debt Payments pursuant to Section 6.09(a)' — meaning RDP basket availability flows additively into investment capacity, though this is an investment basket and not directly an RP basket.

4. **Ratio baskets:** The ratio RP basket (Section 6.06(o)) provides unlimited dividend capacity if First Lien Leverage Ratio ≤ 5.75x (or no worse than closing ratio) on a pro forma basis. This is not a reallocation from another basket but an independent unlimited-conditional basket.

No explicit basket_reallocates_to link data was provided in the norm edges beyond the fungible/reallocable flags noted above, but the `capacity_composition: 'fungible'` on the RP basket and matching dollar caps strongly indicate the RP and RDP general baskets share a single pool.
reasoning.interactions: The builder/Cumulative Amount basket is shared across RP (Section 6.06), RDP (Section 6.09(a)(A)), and investments (Section 6.03(x)) — usage under any one prong reduces availability under the others (`reduced_by_rp_usage`, `reduced_by_rdp_usage`, `reduced_by_investment_usage` all true). The general RP basket (Section 6.06(j)) and general RDP basket (Section 6.09(a)(I)) share a fungible pool at the same dollar cap (greater of $130M / 100% EBITDA), so spending one reduces the other. The investment basket Section 6.03(y) absorbs RDP basket capacity additively into its own cap formula.
reasoning.conclusion: Yes — the general RDP basket (Section 6.09(a)(I)) and the general RP basket (Section 6.06(j)) share a fungible pool (greater of $130M / 100% EBITDA), and the Cumulative Amount/builder basket is shared across RPs, RDPs, and investments simultaneously, with usage under any prong reducing capacity under the others.

stage2.answer: Yes, there are three key reallocation mechanisms: (1) The general RDP basket [Section 6.09(a)(I), p.200] (greater of $130M / 100% EBITDA) and the general RP basket [Section 6.06(j), p.196] (same cap) are fungible — they share a single pool, so capacity not used for debt prepayments is available for dividends/restricted payments and vice versa. (2) The Cumulative Amount (builder basket) [Definition of Cumulative Amount, p.32] is expressly available for RPs, RDPs, and investments from the same shared pool, with usage under any one prong reducing capacity under the others (`reduced_by_rp_usage`, `reduced_by_rdp_usage`, and `reduced_by_investment_usage` are all true); the builder RDP basket [Section 6.09(a)(A), p.199] explicitly `shares_with_rp_builder`. (3) The general investment basket [Section 6.03(y)] incorporates all amounts available for RDPs under Section 6.09(a) as an additive component of its cap, though this flows into investment capacity rather than directly back into RP capacity. Additionally, unlimited RP capacity is available on a ratio basis [Section 6.06(o), p.198] if First Lien Leverage Ratio is ≤ 5.75x (or no worse than the closing ratio) on a pro forma basis.

questions.gold_answer: Yes, under 6.06(j) amount available for Restricted Debt Payment under 6.09(a) and amounts available for Investments under 6.03(y) can be reallocated to the making of Dividends. 6.09(a) includes the greater of $130,000,000 and 100% of Consolidated EBITDA. 6.09(a) also includes other more tailored baskets available for Restricted Debt Payments which may or may not be available for reallocation, including intercompany debt payments and payments in connection with a reorganization or IPO.

reasoning.issue: The question asks whether asset sale proceeds can be used to fund dividend payments under the restricted payments covenant.
reasoning.analysis: The key mechanism is the Cumulative Amount (builder basket) definition. Norm 6e76ed06_builder_source_retained_asset_sale_proceeds establishes that Net Cash Proceeds from asset sales that are retained after the mandatory sweep (i.e., not required to be applied to prepay Term Loans under Section 2.10(c)) flow into clause (f) of the Cumulative Amount as an additive source. The proceeds_flow confirms this: retained asset sale proceeds (after leverage-based sweep tier exemptions or below de minimis thresholds) build the Cumulative Amount. Once in the Cumulative Amount, norm 6e76ed06_builder_usage_permission permits that accumulated capacity to be used for dividend payments (make_dividend_payment is an explicit action_scope). The sweep tier structure determines how much is retained: at First Lien Leverage Ratio > 5.75x, 100% must be swept; between 5.50x and 5.75x, 50% must be swept; at or below 5.50x, 0% is swept (all proceeds are retained and can feed the builder). Additionally, an annual de minimis of the greater of $40,000,000 and 0.3% of assets, and an individual de minimis of 0.3% of assets, exempts certain sales entirely from the sweep. Retained proceeds entering the Cumulative Amount are then available to fund dividends under the builder basket (norm 6e76ed06_builder_usage_permission), subject to that basket being reduced by prior RP, RDP, and investment usage. Separate dividend baskets (6e76ed06_general_rp_basket_permission at $130M / 100% EBITDA, and 6e76ed06_ratio_rp_basket_permission at unlimited if First Lien Leverage ≤ 5.75x) are independent general or conditional permissions for dividends but are not directly funded by asset sale proceeds — they exist independently of proceeds flows.
reasoning.interactions: The asset sale proceeds flow exclusively into norm 6e76ed06_builder_source_retained_asset_sale_proceeds (child_index 6, aggregation_function: sum), which contributes additively to 6e76ed06_builder_usage_permission (the Cumulative Amount). The builder basket is reduced by prior RP, RDP, and investment usage (reduced_by_rp_usage: true, reduced_by_rdp_usage: true, reduced_by_investment_usage: true). The sweep tiers (provision_level_entities) gate how much of the asset sale proceeds survive to enter the builder. No defeaters apply to the retained-proceeds builder source.
reasoning.conclusion: Yes — asset sale proceeds that are retained after the mandatory sweep (i.e., not required to prepay Term Loans under Section 2.10(c)) accumulate in the Cumulative Amount and can be used to make dividends under the builder basket, subject to the leverage-based sweep tier and de minimis thresholds that determine the retained portion.

stage2.answer: Yes, asset sale proceeds can be used to fund dividends, but only to the extent they are retained after the mandatory prepayment sweep. Under the Cumulative Amount definition [Definition of Cumulative Amount, p.32], Net Cash Proceeds from asset sales that are not required to prepay Term Loans (per Section 2.10(c)) are an additive source (clause (f)) of the builder basket, which expressly permits dividend payments [Definition of Cumulative Amount / builder_usage_permission, p.32]. How much is retained depends on the leverage-based sweep tiers [Definition of Applicable Net Cash Proceeds Percentage]: 100% swept if First Lien Leverage Ratio > 5.75x; 50% swept if between 5.50x and 5.75x; 0% swept (all retained) if ≤ 5.50x — plus an annual de minimis exemption of the greater of $40,000,000 and 0.3% of assets and an individual de minimis of 0.3% of assets [Definition of Prepayable Net Cash Proceeds, p.71]. The available Cumulative Amount is reduced by prior restricted payments, restricted debt payments, and investment usage, and dividends may also be made under the separate $130M / 100% EBITDA general basket [Section 6.06(j), p.196] or the unlimited ratio basket if First Lien Leverage Ratio ≤ 5.75x [Section 6.06(o), p.198], neither of which is conditioned on asset sale proceeds.

citations.quote: "Cumulative Amount" shall mean, on any date of determination (the "Reference Date"), the sum of (without duplication):
"Cumulative Amount" shall mean, on any date of determination (the "Reference Date"), the sum of (without duplication):

additional Dividends may be made, directly or indirectly, by any Group Member to Holdings and/or (without duplication) Holdings' direct or indirect equity holders in an aggregate amount not to exceed the greater of $130,000,000 and 100% of Consolidated EBITDA
unlimited additional Dividends; provided that on a Pro Forma Basis, the First Lien Leverage Ratio...shall be no greater than the greater of (x) 5.75 to 1.00

questions.gold_answer: Yes, Retained Asset Sale Proceeds build the Cumulative Amount which consists of proceeds from: Net Cash Proceeds from asset sales not subject to prepayment on account of Section 2.10(c)(iv), permitting proceeds from any Asset Sale using the unlimited basket 6.05(z) if such Asset Sale is a sale of a product line and the pro forma First Lien Net Leverage Ratio is 6.25x or less or if such test is no worse pro forma. Also includes asset sale proceeds not swept when First Lien Net Leverage Ratio is 5.75x or less (50% of proceeds) or 5.50x or less (100% of proceeds). Also includes Net Cash Proceeds from non-collateral assets, ordinary course asset sales, asset sales from non-ratio baskets, casualty events, and proceeds from collateral assets below de minimis thresholds of $20M/15% EBITDA individual and $40M/30% EBITDA annual.

reasoning.issue: The question asks for the total amount of quantifiable dividend capacity across all applicable baskets under the restricted payments covenant.
reasoning.analysis:
Per category guidance, baskets must be classified and only general_purpose baskets summed; restricted_purpose, unlimited_conditional, and categorical baskets must be listed separately.

**Step 1: Classify all baskets**

1. Builder Basket (Cumulative Amount) — capacity_category: general_purpose. Dollar floor = greater of $130M and 100% of Consolidated EBITDA (starter), plus 50% of cumulative CNI, plus retained ECF, plus 100% of equity proceeds, plus retained asset sale proceeds, plus investment returns, plus debt conversion proceeds. The starter uses a greatest_of with the three-test aggregate (greatest of 50% CNI, retained ECF, or 140% of Consolidated EBITDA). The dollar floor of the starter is $130M / 100% EBITDA. The builder basket is reduced by RP, RDP, and investment usage and is shared with the builder RDP basket. At closing, the minimum quantifiable floor is **$130,000,000** (or 100% of LTM EBITDA if larger), growing over time.

2. General RP Basket (§6.06(j)) — capacity_category: general_purpose. Cap = greater of **$130,000,000** and 100% of Consolidated EBITDA for most recently ended Test Period. capacity_composition: fungible (meaning the $130M floor is its own independent pool — no shares_capacity_pool link to the builder basket is recorded in the data).

3. Management Equity Basket (§6.06(b)) — capacity_category: restricted_purpose. Annual cap = greater of **$20,000,000** and 15% of Consolidated EBITDA; carryforward permitted. Restricted to repurchases from current/former officers, directors, consultants, employees.

4. Ratio RP Basket (§6.06(o)) — capacity_category: unlimited_conditional. Unlimited dividends if, pro forma, First Lien Leverage Ratio ≤ 5.75x OR no-worse test satisfied. Not a fixed dollar amount — excluded from the quantifiable sum.

5. Tax Distribution Basket (§6.06(c)) — capacity_category: restricted_purpose / categorical. No fixed dollar cap in extracted data.

6. Holdco Overhead Basket (§6.06(k)) — capacity_category: restricted_purpose / categorical. No fixed dollar cap.

7. Equity Award Basket (§6.06(d)) — capacity_category: restricted_purpose / unlimited_on_condition. Covers cashless exercise/tax withholding; no fixed dollar cap.

8. Unrestricted Sub Distribution Basket (§6.06(p)) — capacity_category: categorical. Covers distribution of unsub equity/assets; amount depends on unsub values.

**Step 2: Sum general_purpose baskets (fixed dollar floors only)**

- Builder Basket floor: $130,000,000
- General RP Basket floor: $130,000,000
- No shares_capacity_pool link between these two exists in the data, so they are independent pools.

Total quantifiable general_purpose floor = **$130M + $130M = $260,000,000**

Plus (restricted_purpose, quantifiable): Management Equity Basket = **$20,000,000** per year (greater-of floor), listed separately.

The builder basket and general RP basket also each grow with 100% of LTM EBITDA if that exceeds $130M, making total capacity above $260M in any period where EBITDA > $130M.
reasoning.interactions: The builder basket (6e76ed06_builder_usage_permission) is shared with the builder RDP basket (6e76ed06_builder_rdp_basket_permission) — usage under either reduces the Cumulative Amount (reduced_by_rdp_usage: true, reduced_by_investment_usage: true). The general RP basket (6e76ed06_general_rp_basket_permission) has capacity_composition: fungible but no recorded shares_capacity_pool link to the builder basket, so they are treated as independent pools. No defeaters are present. The ratio basket (6e76ed06_ratio_rp_basket_permission) is unlimited_conditional and therefore not addable to the quantifiable sum.
reasoning.conclusion: The minimum quantifiable dividend capacity at closing (dollar floors) is $260,000,000 from two independent general_purpose baskets ($130M builder starter + $130M general RP basket), each growing with 100% of LTM Consolidated EBITDA if that exceeds $130M; additionally, up to $20,000,000 per year (or 15% of EBITDA if greater) is available under the restricted_purpose management equity basket for employee/director equity repurchases.

stage2.answer: The total quantifiable dividend capacity comprises two independent general_purpose basket floors aggregating **$260,000,000** at closing: (1) the Cumulative Amount (builder basket) with a starter floor of the greater of $130,000,000 and 100% of Consolidated EBITDA for the most recently ended Test Period [Definition of Cumulative Amount, p.32], which also grows with 50% of cumulative CNI, retained ECF, equity proceeds, retained asset sale proceeds, investment returns, and debt conversion proceeds (subject to reduction for RP, RDP, and investment usage); and (2) the General RP Basket permitting dividends up to the greater of $130,000,000 and 100% of Consolidated EBITDA [Section 6.06(j), p.196], which is an independent pool with no recorded shared-capacity link to the builder basket. Additionally, a restricted_purpose Management Equity Basket permits up to the greater of $20,000,000 per year and 15% of Consolidated EBITDA for repurchases from current/former officers, directors, consultants, and employees (with carryforward) [Section 6.06(b), p.195]. The ratio basket [Section 6.06(o), p.198] provides unlimited dividend capacity if First Lien Leverage Ratio is ≤ 5.75x pro forma (or no-worse), but its capacity is not a fixed dollar amount and is excluded from the quantifiable sum; categorical baskets (tax distributions, holdco overhead, equity award cashless exercises, unrestricted subsidiary distributions) are separately available for their specific purposes without fixed dollar caps in the extracted data.

questions.gold_answer: $520m (or 409.9% of EBITDA) plus all assets that do not secure the Loans and all non-EBITDA producing assets. RP starter: $130m/100% EBITDA. General RP basket: $130m/100% EBITDA. General prepayment of debt basket: $130m/100% EBITDA. General investment basket: $130m/100% EBITDA.

reasoning.issue: Whether the Borrower can dividend a $200m asset/business division (with negative EBITDA) to shareholders when the First Lien Net Leverage Ratio is 6.0x, and which RP baskets are available at that leverage level.
reasoning.analysis:
There are four potential pathways to analyze:

1. RATIO BASKET (Section 6.06(o)) — unlimited dividends are permitted if, on a Pro Forma Basis, the First Lien Leverage Ratio is no greater than the GREATER OF (x) 5.75x and (y) the ratio determined WITHOUT giving effect to the Subject Transactions (i.e., the 'no-worse' test). The absolute threshold (prong x) requires ≤5.75x. At 6.0x pre-transaction, the Borrower fails prong (x). CRITICAL: The no-worse test (prong y) compares the pro forma ratio to the pre-transaction ratio of 6.0x. Because the business being dividended has NEGATIVE EBITDA, removing it from the consolidated group INCREASES consolidated EBITDA (a negative contribution is eliminated), which DECREASES the leverage ratio pro forma. Therefore, the pro forma ratio after the dividend will be LOWER than the pre-transaction 6.0x. Since the no-worse test requires the pro forma ratio to be no greater than the pre-transaction ratio (6.0x), and the pro forma ratio will be below 6.0x (EBITDA improves), the no-worse test IS SATISFIED. The ratio basket is available via the no-worse prong.

2. GENERAL RP BASKET (Section 6.06(j)) — permits dividends up to the greater of $130m and 100% of Consolidated EBITDA, with no leverage condition. A $200m non-cash dividend (distributing an asset) would need to fit within this cap. However, there is no explicit indication that this basket is capped at fair market value for non-cash dividends, so the $200m asset distribution may exceed the $130m floor. This basket provides at least $130m of capacity (or more if Consolidated EBITDA exceeds $130m), but may be insufficient alone for a $200m distribution.

3. BUILDER BASKET (Definition of Cumulative Amount) — general purpose basket with no standalone leverage condition, providing capacity based on accumulated sources. No leverage test required to use. Available to the extent the Cumulative Amount has been built up, but actual capacity is not quantified in the data.

4. UNSUB DISTRIBUTION BASKET (Section 6.06(p)) — categorical permission, but only covers distribution of assets of an Unrestricted Subsidiary. The question posits a business division/asset of the Borrower itself, not an Unrestricted Subsidiary, so this basket is not directly applicable unless the division is first designated as an Unrestricted Subsidiary (subject to the unsub designation rules and the $40m investment cap under Section 6.03(s)).

CONCLUSION ON RATIO BASKET: The no-worse test is the decisive pathway. Removing a negative-EBITDA business improves the consolidated leverage ratio pro forma, satisfying the no-worse prong of Section 6.06(o).
reasoning.interactions: The ratio basket (6e76ed06_ratio_rp_basket_permission) has a condition_tree with an OR structure: prong (x) absolute ≤5.75x threshold OR prong (y) pro forma no-worse test. Only one prong need be satisfied. The general RP basket (6e76ed06_general_rp_basket_permission) is independent with its own cap. The builder basket (6e76ed06_builder_usage_permission) is general purpose and fungible with the general RP basket (both capacity_category: general_purpose). No defeaters are listed in the data.
reasoning.conclusion: The Borrower can dividend the negative-EBITDA asset/business division at 6.0x leverage under the ratio basket (Section 6.06(o)) via the no-worse test, because removing a negative-EBITDA asset improves the pro forma leverage ratio below the pre-transaction 6.0x level. Additionally, the general RP basket (Section 6.06(j)) provides at least $130m of unconditional capacity, and the builder basket may provide further capacity depending on accumulated amounts.

stage2.answer: Yes, the Borrower can likely dividend the $200m negative-EBITDA asset/business division to shareholders at 6.0x First Lien Net Leverage via the ratio basket in Section 6.06(o), which permits unlimited dividends if, on a Pro Forma Basis, the First Lien Leverage Ratio is no greater than the greater of (x) 5.75x or (y) the pre-transaction ratio — because removing a negative-EBITDA business from the consolidated group increases consolidated EBITDA, thereby reducing the pro forma leverage ratio below the pre-transaction 6.0x level and satisfying the no-worse prong (y) [Section 6.06(o), p. 198]. Separately, the general RP basket under Section 6.06(j) provides unconditional capacity of at least the greater of $130m and 100% of Consolidated EBITDA (no leverage condition), which may cover a portion of the distribution [Section 6.06(j), p. 196]. The builder basket (Cumulative Amount, p. 32) provides additional general-purpose capacity to the extent it has been built up, also without a leverage condition. Note: if the $200m distribution is structured as a non-cash dividend of a business division, the Borrower should confirm whether the value of the distribution is measured at fair market value for purposes of basket sizing under Sections 6.06(j) and the builder basket.

questions.gold_answer: Yes, because the Ratio RP basket 6.06(o) permits such transaction as long as the First Lien Net Leverage Ratio, even if above 5.75x, is no worse.
